sections: What Our Full Unpacking Service Actually Covers

Our full unpacking service is the natural conclusion of a properly managed move — the point where a houseful of boxes becomes a home you can live in straight away. When our Sussex team arrives, we don’t simply set crates down in the hallway and leave. We work room by room, opening every box, lifting each item out, and placing it where it belongs. Crockery goes back into cupboards, books onto shelves, clothes into wardrobes and drawers, and kitchenware into the right working zones around the hob, sink and prep areas. Because we packed many of these boxes ourselves through our full packing service, we already know what is inside each one and exactly which room it was lifted from, so nothing ends up in the wrong place.

By the time we finish, your beds are made, your sofas and tables are positioned, your wardrobes are hung, and the kettle is plugged in and ready. It is the difference between a house full of cardboard and a home you can genuinely relax in on your very first evening — and for many of the West and East Sussex families we look after, that first calm evening is exactly why they booked us.

Unpacking the small items is only half the job. A home doesn’t feel finished until the big pieces are standing where they should be and ready to use. Our crews reassemble the furniture we dismantled during your house removals — bed frames, wardrobes, dining tables, bookcases, desks and flat-pack units — using the fixings we carefully bagged and labelled at your old address. We level wardrobe doors, tighten bed slats and check every joint before we move on, so you never inherit a wobbly table or a drawer that won’t close.

Once the structure is up, we dress the rooms. Mattresses are positioned, beds are made with fresh linen if you’d like, mirrors and pictures are stood ready for hanging, and lamps and soft furnishings are placed so each room looks lived-in rather than left mid-move. Typical reassembly and placement work includes:

  • Rebuilding bed frames, wardrobes, drawer units and flat-pack furniture
  • Positioning sofas, dining sets, desks and bookcases exactly where you want them
  • Making up beds so they’re ready for that all-important first night’s sleep
  • Reconnecting freestanding appliances such as washing machines where access allows
  • Standing mirrors, artwork and shelving ready for fixing to the wall
Packing Materials Removed & Taken Away the Same Day

Nothing dampens the excitement of a new home faster than a living room buried under flattened boxes and a mountain of bubble wrap. With our full unpacking service, all of that disappears before we leave. As we empty each box we break it down, gather the packing paper, bubble wrap and protective blankets, and clear every offcut so your floors are walkable and your surfaces are clear. You won’t spend your first weekend ferrying cardboard to the tip or wrestling it into the recycling.

We take the bulk of the packaging away the same day, and because we’re a local Sussex firm constantly out on the road, we collect any used boxes and materials left behind whenever one of our vans is next in your area. It’s a tidy, low-waste finish that keeps usable packaging in circulation rather than in landfill. If you’d like to understand the quality of materials we use throughout, our packing materials page explains the double-walled cartons, acid-free tissue and specialist cartons that protect your belongings from the first box to the last.
Who Benefits Most From a Full Unpack

A full unpack is for anyone who simply doesn’t have the time, energy or inclination to spend days surrounded by boxes — and in practice that covers a lot of people. Busy professionals heading back to work the morning after completion can walk into an organised home rather than a building site. Families with young children avoid the stress of hunting for the right box while juggling school runs and bedtimes. We unpack a great many moves for older clients and those with limited mobility, for whom lifting, reaching and bending over heavy cartons simply isn’t practical or safe — our team does every bit of that for them.

It is equally valuable for long-distance and international relocations, where you may arrive tired after a long journey, or even fly in days after your belongings, to find everything already in place. Whatever the circumstances, the people who benefit most are those who value their time and want to skip straight to enjoying their new home. The groups we most often help include:

  • Working professionals returning to the office the next day
  • Families with children who need normality restored quickly
  • Elderly clients and anyone with reduced mobility
  • Relocations from across the UK and Europe arriving after a long trip
  • Anyone who would rather we handled the unpacking from start to finish
How Unpacking Works Alongside Our Packing Service

The full unpacking service works best as the closing chapter of a complete, end-to-end move — and it is most efficient when the same team packs and unpacks. When we carry out your professional packing at the old property, we label every carton by room and contents and keep a clear inventory. That same record guides the unpack at the other end, so boxes go straight to the correct room and items return to logical, sensible places rather than being dumped wherever there’s a gap.

It dovetails neatly with our other services too. Delicate items wrapped under our fragile packing service — china, glassware, mirrors and ceramics — are unwrapped with the same care and set out safely. Clients who want the very highest level of finish often pair the unpack with our white-glove service, and anything you’re not ready to place can be diverted to our secure storage while the rest of your home is set up. It all flows as one continuous, coordinated process from your first box to your final cup of tea.

Wolves Removals is a family-run Sussex removals and storage company based at Doryln House, London Road, Ashington, near Pulborough, RH20 3JT. Since 2016 we’ve been packing, moving and unpacking homes across West and East Sussex, Surrey, Hampshire and Kent, as well as handling moves throughout the UK and into Europe. Unpacking a home properly takes genuine experience — knowing how to handle antiques and fine pieces, how to place furniture so a room works, and how to leave a property spotless — and that is the standard our crews bring to every job.

We’re a LAPADA member and Checkatrade-verified, fully insured with £10m liability cover, and recommended by respected local agents including Fine & Country, Justin Lloyd and Mansell McTaggart. You can read more about our story and values on our about us page, and when you’re ready to plan your move we’ll happily build the unpack into a single tailored quote. Call us on 01903 893731, email contact@wolves-removals.co.uk, or request a quote online to get started.
faqs: Do you take away all the packing materials on the same day?

Yes. As we unpack each box we flatten the cardboard and collect all the paper, bubble wrap and protective blankets, clearing it from your rooms so your home is tidy before we leave. We remove the bulk of it the same day, and because we’re a local Sussex firm we collect any remaining used boxes and packaging whenever one of our vans is next in your area, keeping good materials in circulation.
Will you reassemble furniture and make up the beds?

We do. Our crews rebuild the furniture we dismantled during the move — bed frames, wardrobes, tables and flat-pack units — using the fixings we labelled and bagged at your old address. We position everything where you want it and, if you’d like, make up the beds with fresh linen so they’re ready for your first night’s sleep in the new home.
Can I have the full unpack as part of one complete move?

Absolutely — that’s how it works best. We combine our full packing service, removal and unpacking into one coordinated job, so the same team that labelled your boxes by room also unpacks them at the other end. We’ll build the unpack into a single tailored quote when you request a quote, with no need to arrange anything separately.
Is the unpacking service suitable for elderly or less mobile clients?

Yes, it’s one of the most popular reasons clients book it. Our team does all the lifting, reaching, bending and placing, so there’s no need for you to handle a single heavy carton. We work at a comfortable pace, put everything within easy reach, and make sure your home is safe and clear before we leave. Just call 01903 893731 to talk through what you need.
